Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Mura-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Japan)]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} | rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | founded = {{ill|Jimmu period|ja|神武天皇}} | architecture = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festival = {{ill|May 20|ja|5月20日}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}}, the shrine dates back to the {{ill|Jimmu period|ja|神武天皇}}. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the {{ill|Divine Mirror|ja|神鏡}} and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮}}. Although not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, the legendary ascetic {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角}} used {{ill|Tomogashima Island|ja|友ヶ島}} as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}} tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the {{ill|Kitan Strait|ja|紀淡海峡}}, the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Mura-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Japan)]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=加太春日神社 | 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]加太1342 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌神]]
[[経津主神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[村社]] | 創建=[[神武天皇]]時代 | 本殿=一間社[[流造]] | 例祭=[[5月20日]] | 画像=[[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿（重要文化財）]] '''加太春日神社'''（かだかすがじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]加太にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 *[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神]] *[[経津主神]] ; 配祀神 *[[天照大神]] *[[住吉三神|住吉大神]] == 歴史 == 創建年代は明確ではないが、紀伊国造家旧記によると、[[神武天皇]][[神武東征|東征]]の際、[[紀国造]]氏の祖である天道根命が神鏡と日矛の二つの[[神宝]]を奉じて加太浦に上陸し、頓宮を造営して天照大御神を祀ったことに始まるという（神鏡・日矛は[[日前神宮・国懸神宮]]の神体である）。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はない（式外社）が、「[[紀伊国神名帳]]」 に「正一位春日大神」と記されている。[[役小角]]（飛鳥時代の半ば伝説的な山岳修業者で修験道の開祖、役行者とも称す）が、[[友ヶ島]]を行場とし、当社を勧請して守護神としたという。そのため現在でも毎年四月二十日、当社に聖護院門跡が大勢の山伏僧と共に参拝している（[[聖護院]]は[[本山修験宗]]の総本山）。 当地は[[紀淡海峡]]に面して漁業の盛んなところであるため、のちに航海安全と大漁を祈願する住吉大神が合祀されたものと見られ、中世には「住吉神社」とも称されていた。[[文保]]元年（[[1317年]]）6月29日附「預所某田地寄進状」（向井家文書）にある 「賀太庄住吉社」は当社のこととされる。 「紀伊風土記」によると、日野左衛門藤原光福が地頭としてこの地を支配した[[嘉元]]年間（[[1303年]]-[[1317年]]）に、自分の祖先神である春日三神を新たに祀り、総名として「春日社」と称したとあり、[[嘉吉]]2年（[[1442年]]）の記録に「春日明神神事日記」（向井家文書）がある。 紀伊名所図会によると、社地はもと、現在地から東の山の中腹にあったが、[[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1592年]]）に[[豊臣秀長]]の家臣で和歌山[[城代]]（当時、秀長は大和郡山に居城、大和、和泉、紀伊三国を領し、紀伊国は和歌山城代が支配）の[[桑山重晴]]によって現在地に遷したと記している。なお、[[棟札]]（重要文化財の附指定）によって[[慶長]]元年（[[1596年]]）に大がかりな社殿の造作がなされたことも実証されている。 当社は、明治時代まで神職はおかず、神社経営は[[宮座]]形式の当屋制によって運営されていた。 第二次大戦後、神社が国家の保護を離れたことにより当社が[[宗教法人]]となったときより、現社名の「加太春日神社」が用いられるようになった。 昭和56年、環境庁（現[[環境省]]）主催の第23回自然公園大会において[[採火神社]]に指定され、聖火を献火すると共に、氏子有志による獅子舞が郷土芸能として披露された。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（建造物） - 一間社[[流造]]。[[慶長]]元年（[[1596年]]）造営。1931年（昭和6年）12月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2822|加太春日神社本殿}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ 加太春日神社 和歌山市の文化財]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html 加太春日神社] - 公式サイト {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かたかすかしんしや}} [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]]

[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]]

These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]]

In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]]

{{Shrine | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

{{Shrine | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Azuchi–Momoyama period architecture]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kada, Wakayama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

{{Shrine | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Kii Province Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

{{Shrine | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮}}. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:和歌山市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}
