revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398578]] Wikidata name: Kada Kasuga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398578]] Wikidata name: Kada Kasuga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|30.7|N|135|4|28.9|E|region:JP-30_scale:20000_type:landmark|name=加太春日神社|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg|220px]] }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg | prefecture = {{ill|Wakayama|qid=Q200747}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690}} }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398578]] Wikidata name: Kada Kasuga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398578]] Wikidata name: Kada Kasuga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.275194444444445|N|135.07469444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg | location = {{ill|Wakayama|qid=Q200747}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690}} }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg | location = {{ill|Wakayama|qid=Q200747}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690}} }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}}

Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[sn:Kada Kasuga Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}}

{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg | location = {{ill|Wakayama|qid=Q200747}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690}} }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}}

Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}}

{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg | location = {{ill|Wakayama|qid=Q200747}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690}} }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}}

[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg | location = {{ill|Wakayama|WD=Q200747}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|qq=|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|qq=|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|qq=|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}}

As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kada-kasuga-jinja]][[ja:加太春日神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398578]] Wikidata name: Kada Kasuga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398578]] Wikidata name: Kada Kasuga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kada Kasuga Shrine (加太春日神社) | location = {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.275194444444445|N|135.07469444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) - debated origin
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]] | architecture = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 20|May 20]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kada-kasuga-jinja.jpg | location = {{ill|Wakayama|WD=Q200747}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}} }} [[File:Kada-kasuga-jinja honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Kada Kasuga Shrine''' (加太春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kada, Wakayama City|ja|和歌山市加太|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kada, Wakayama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}}

It holds historical and cultural significance as a former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == ; Primary deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] ; Additional deities * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} == History == The shrine's foundation is uncertain, but according to records from the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604953}}, the shrine dates back to the [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu period]]. It is said that Tendo-ne-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Kii-no-Kuni clan, carried the sacred treasures—the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|Divine Mirror]] and the {{ill|Sun Spear|ja|日矛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Spear|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—to Kada Bay, where a temporary palace was constructed to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}. These treasures were later venerated as the sacred objects of {{ill|Hichimen Shrine|ja|日前神宮・国懸神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichimen Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日前神宮・國懸神宮|1=Hinokuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q888184}}. Although not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], the shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|ja|紀伊国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-no-Kuni Shrine Register|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17210008}} as "Shoichii Kasuga Daimyojin" (正一位春日大神). During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] used [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima Island]] as a sacred training ground and enshrined the shrine's guardian deity there. As a result, the shrine remains connected to the practices of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] tradition. In later years, due to the area's prominence as a fishing village along the [[:en:Kitan Strait|Kitan Strait]], the shrine began to include {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} as a deity for maritime safety and abundant catches. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it became known as "Sumiyoshi Shrine." Historical documents, such as a 1317 land dedication from the Mukai family archives, refer to the shrine as "Kada Manor Sumiyoshi Shrine." In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], Fujiwara descendants introduced the {{ill|Kasuga deities|ja|春日三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, naming the shrine "Kasuga Shrine." Historical records such as the "Kasuga Myojin Festival Diary" (1442) affirm this name. The shrine's current location is due to relocation ordered by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s vassal {{ill|Kuwahara Shigeharu|ja|桑山重晴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwahara Shigeharu|1=Kuwayama Shigeharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537542}} during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}}. Extensive reconstructions occurred in 1596, as evidenced by a {{ill|Ridge Tag|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Tag|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}}, now designated a National Important Cultural Property. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''': Constructed in 1596, the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] building is designated as a National Important Cultural Property (since December 14, 1931).{{National Cultural Property Database|102|2822|Kada Kasuga Shrine Main Hall}}[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/shitei/784/ Kada Kasuga Shrine, Wakayama City Cultural Properties] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hir.ne.jp/e3/omiya/index.html Kada Kasuga Shrine Official Site] (in Japanese) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加太春日神社|version=88235820|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:加太春日神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] {{Commons category|Kada-kasuga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11398578}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
